Organic farming brings variety and challenges for Olsons - marshallindependent.com “We have been farming together since 1988,” Olson said. “I got asked on some food-grade soybeans, ‘Are these organic?’ And we had no idea what that meant,” Olson said. Raising organic crops means following a lot of different rules, Olson said. Part of the work of organic farming includes undergoing inspections, and keeping documentation for inspectors, Olson said. “So the soybeans and the small grains that we grow, they are for seed production,” Olson said. Marketing organic crops is also a different process, Olson said. “Marketing for organics is much more relational,” and involves meeting with potential buyers, Olson said. A variety of factors can impact the market for organic grains, including consumer demand and animal health concerns for livestock producers, Olson said. The number of farmers who have tried organic farming has ebbed and flowed over the years, Olson said. While organic farming is a lot of hard work, Carolyn Olson said she and Jonathan enjoy it. Jonathan and I still enjoy farming together,” Olson said. https://www.marshallindependent.com/news/local-news/2026/03/organic-farming-brings-variety-and-challenges-for-olsons/
